signals.reasoning: BTC is already in a liquidation-flush state, so I would not force a fresh position while veto is active.
ETH is already flushing and does not offer a clean enough new edge in either direction right here.
SOL is already in flush territory and the board itself flags it no-trade, so I would wait rather than force it.
IP has some squeeze fuel from negative funding, but liquidity is too poor and the board itself flags it no-trade.
RESOLV is one of the better reduced-size squeeze longs because funding is clearly negative while price is still holding up better than the broader tape.
TNSR is already weak and too illiquid to trust for a serious fresh short entry after the move.
ZEREBRO is already in a flush state and too illiquid to trust for a serious fresh short chase.
BLAST is a reduced-size squeeze long because funding is deeply negative while price is still positive, but the short-covering character and poor liquidity keep confidence restrained.
YGG is too flat and too low-liquidity to justify a fresh directional trade.
0G remains the strongest squeeze-long candidate because funding is still deeply negative while price remains strong, though the move is already extended and liquidity is poor.
GOAT is already deeply flushed and too illiquid to trust for a fresh short chase.
